key_points: Regional culture is the way of life, traditions, and customs of a specific area
Culture includes language, food, clothing, festivals, art, music, and dance
Language helps preserve local stories, folk tales, and values
Festivals and rituals celebrate traditions and strengthen community bonds
Trade and migration spread ideas, crafts, and cultural practices
examples: Bihu dance in Assam celebrates agricultural festivals
Tenali Raman stories show South Indian culture and humor
Konark Sun Temple in Odisha reflects local religious beliefs and art
Durga Puja in West Bengal and Pongal in Tamil Nadu are region-specific festivals
Barter trade between communities spread crafts and ideas
formulas: Regional Culture → Language + Art + Music + Dance + Festivals + Traditions
Trade + Migration → Spread of ideas and cultural practices
exam_tips: Remember examples of regional art, music, and festivals
Understand the importance of language and stories in preserving culture
Focus on how trade and migration influenced regional practices
Learn the difference between local and national cultural practices
